Smyth was then proprictor and editor, a description of this engmnng,
with a copy of the verses, appeared on the 19th September, 1863.

The verses were published in ““The Irish Packet’’ (1903), and in other
papers. They also appeared in ‘‘ The Christian Brothers’ Magazine ™
(1907), with the heading * Lmes written by William Smith O’Brien—
now puh];c;hcd for the first time.’

contextual_chunks.title: The Irish Book Lover, vol. III, no. 5, December 1911
The Irish Book Lover, vol. III, no. 5, December 1911
contextual_chunks.text:
THE IRISH BOOK LOVER 87

BOOK AUCTIONS.

A bound copy of the first volume of the ‘‘ Irish Book Lover’ was
auctioned at the salerooms of Messrs. James Adams and Sons, Dublin,
in the middle of November and knocked down at 9s. 6d. A very high
figure, especially for a periodical so recently established. This is the
frst instance of the ‘‘ I.B.I..”” coming under the hammer,

Dublin. | R. S. M.

Messrs. Bennett and Son, Upr. Ormond Quay, Dublin, sold a large
collection on 7th November. The titles and prices realised of some of

the works were as follows:—Trial of T. C. Luby, 1s. 6d.; Irish Penny
Vagazine, 1833, 1s. €a.; Petrie’s Ecclesiastical Architecture, 1845, 14s. ;
Barringtou’s Historic Memoirs, 2 vols., 5s.; The Tribune, a Dublin
Weekly, Nos. 1-14, 1855-56, 5s. 6d.; The Irish People, Nos. 1-43, 1863-
85, 6s. 6d.; Madden’s United Irishmen, 1lst ed., 7 vols., 34s.; second
'ed., 4 vols., £2 6s.; The Grand Juries of the County of Westmeath,
from 1727 till 1853, with Historical Appendix, compiled, pfinted and
bound by J. C. Lyons, Ledestown,; 1853, only 25 copies printed, rare, -
£3 5s.; Catalogue of Lough Fea Library, privately printed, 1872, 25s. ;
De Burgo’s H
